text.title: GraphQL API Design Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Schema and resolver mistakes: N+1 without DataLoader, missing pagination, throwing instead of returning errors.

antipattern.description: Resolver eagerly loads all related entities in the parent query, regardless of whether the client requested them.
antipattern.reason: Executes DB queries for data the client never uses, degrading performance proportionally to the number of unused relations loaded.

antipattern.description: List field returns all items with no pagination: `async def users(self) -> List[User]`.
antipattern.reason: Returns millions of rows in one request on a large dataset. All list fields that could grow must use cursor-based Connection types with server-enforced size caps.

antipattern.description: Mutations raise exceptions for business errors (validation, not found, conflict) instead of returning typed error payloads.
antipattern.reason: Exceptions become opaque `errors[]` entries with no type information. Clients cannot distinguish "user not found" from "permission denied" without string-matching error messages.

antipattern.description: Schema breaking changes (renaming or removing a field) merged without a deprecation cycle and schema diff check in CI.
antipattern.reason: Clients that query the renamed/removed field receive a GraphQL error. Breaking changes require `@deprecated(reason: "...")`, usage analytics to confirm zero traffic, then removal.
